task_completion.description: Verify that user accounts for both tenants have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Emoncms Administration or Users page showing 'tenant_a' (tenant_a@building.local) and 'tenant_b' (tenant_b@building.local) in the user list.

task_completion.description: Verify that energy consumption data inputs are posted for Tenant A.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Emoncms Inputs page (while logged in as tenant_a or admin) showing a node named 'floor_a' with input keys 'hvac_w' and 'lighting_w'.

task_completion.description: Verify that energy consumption data inputs are posted for Tenant B.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Emoncms Inputs page (while logged in as tenant_b or admin) showing a node named 'floor_b' with input keys 'hvac_w' and 'lighting_w'.

task_completion.description: Verify that input process lists are configured to log data to named feeds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Emoncms Feeds page showing active feeds created from the inputs, or the Inputs page showing the process list (wrench icon) configured with 'Log to feed' actions for the hvac_w and lighting_w inputs.

task_completion.description: Verify that a dedicated monitoring dashboard is created for Tenant A.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Emoncms Dashboards list or a dashboard view showing a dashboard explicitly named 'Tenant A Energy'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a dedicated monitoring dashboard is created for Tenant B.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Emoncms Dashboards list or a dashboard view showing a dashboard explicitly named 'Tenant B Energy'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Emoncms web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigated to http://localhost displaying the Emoncms GUI, rather than just terminal outputs or text editors.

integrity.description: Verify dashboards were created using the Emoncms dashboard builder.
integrity.visual_evidence: Dashboards should appear within the standard Emoncms interface with its native styling and widgets, not as manually created standalone HTML files.

integrity.description: Verify data was posted via API or UI, not by direct database manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of curl commands, API helper usage, or UI interaction for inputs/feeds, rather than direct SQL queries in a terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Tenant A must have username 'tenant_a' and email 'tenant_a@building.local'. Tenant B must have username 'tenant_b' and email 'tenant_b@building.local'. Tenant A's inputs must be on node 'floor_a' and Tenant B's on node 'floor_b'. Both must have inputs 'hvac_w' and 'lighting_w'. Dashboards must be exactly named 'Tenant A Energy' and 'Tenant B Energy'.
